senses.gloss: to confine (something or someone); to shut, lock, close, or fence in; to enclose
to protect; to guard; to keep; to shelter
to protect; to guard; to keep; to shelter; to raise or treat as a relation (especially as one's child); to adopt
to outspread (one's hands) like a bowl
to supply (an area) with a toilet
the lateral part of the torso, the side
the lateral propelling organ, the wing

etymology.text:
From the root ك ن ف (k n f), with some senses denominally derived from كَنِيف (kanīf, “a toilet”).

From Proto-Semitic *kanap-; cognate with Akkadian 𒉺 (kappuᵐ, “wing, branch”), Hebrew כנף (kanáf, “wing”), Ge'ez ክንፍ (kənf, “wing, fin”).
